key_insight: High-velocity air fuel (HVAF) spraying can produce thick NiTi coatings that retain martensitic transformation and functional shape-memory behavior after annealing.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical path to add wear-resistant, thermally active NiTi functional coatings to steel components where conventional spray or cladding routes have struggled.
method: HVAF-sprayed NiTi onto mild steel using four processing parameter sets
Characterized chemistry, porosity, microstructure, and phase transformation behavior
Tested functional response using 3-point bending, nanoindentation, and scratch loading
evidence_signal: The coatings were evaluated with microstructural and thermomechanical measurements and showed martensitic transformation plus thermal actuation and shape-memory effects after annealing.
watch_item: As-sprayed coatings contain inhomogeneous microstructure, oxides, voids, and high defect/stress levels, so post-spray annealing and adhesion/strength validation are important before deployment.
paper_title: HVAF Spraying of NiTi Coatings: Microstructure, Phase Transformation and Shape Memory Behavior
